_meta.failure_shape: Layer 3 verify-rendered-parity — same element exists in both source trees but at different rendered mount levels
_meta.originating_failure: heirship-app-v2 chrome breadcrumb case: <TaCrumbs /> existed in both candidate (heirship-app-v2) and oracle (heirship-app-v3) source. A source-tree walk via verify-oracle-match would say {matched: true} because the component name is present in both. BUT in the rendered DOM, the candidate mounts <TaCrumbs /> inside [data-testid='page-body'] while the oracle mounts it inside AppShellHeader. Source audit is BLIND to mount-level architectural divergence; verify-rendered-parity is the gate.
_meta.why_source_audit_misses_this: Both candidate and oracle source contain a <TaCrumbs /> import + render. The CONTAINING component differs — heirship-app-v2's TAMatterDetail.tsx body renders <TaCrumbs />, while heirship-app-v3's AppShellLayout renders it inside the header chrome. A component-tree diff (verify-oracle-match) treats both trees as containing TaCrumbs and reports matched. Only the rendered-DOM diff (verify-rendered-parity) sees the parent-path divergence.
